evals.prompt: Set up a Git branching strategy for our team of 5 developers. We deploy to production weekly and need hotfix capability. Use conventional commits. Define branch naming, PR workflow, and merge strategy.
evals.expected_output: Branch strategy (trunk-based or GitFlow), naming conventions, PR template, merge rules

assertions.name: branch_strategy
assertions.description: Clear branching model defined with rationale

assertions.name: naming_convention
assertions.description: Branch naming: feat/xxx, fix/xxx, chore/xxx

assertions.name: conventional_commits
assertions.description: Commit format: type(scope): description

assertions.name: pr_workflow
assertions.description: PR creation, review, and merge process defined

assertions.name: hotfix_process
assertions.description: Emergency hotfix procedure documented

evals.prompt: I'm working on a feature branch and main has diverged significantly. There are merge conflicts in 5 files. Help me resolve the conflicts and get my branch up to date.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step conflict resolution: fetch, rebase, resolve per file, continue, verify

assertions.name: fetch_first
assertions.description: Fetches latest from origin before rebasing

assertions.name: rebase_approach
assertions.description: Uses rebase (not merge) for cleaner history

assertions.name: per_file_resolution
assertions.description: Guides through resolving each conflicted file

assertions.name: verify_after
assertions.description: Runs tests after conflict resolution to verify

assertions.name: force_push
assertions.description: Notes need for force push after rebase

evals.prompt: Write a commit message for: added rate limiting to the API using express-rate-limit, configured different limits for auth endpoints (10/min) vs general endpoints (100/min), and added rate limit headers to responses.
evals.expected_output: Conventional commit message with appropriate type, scope, and description

assertions.name: conventional_format
assertions.description: Uses type(scope): description format

assertions.name: appropriate_type
assertions.description: Uses 'feat' type for new rate limiting feature

assertions.name: concise_subject
assertions.description: Subject line under 72 characters

assertions.name: body_details
assertions.description: Body explains the different rate limits

assertions.name: not_overly_verbose
assertions.description: Focused on what and why, not implementation details

evals.prompt: Set up pre-commit hooks for our project: lint (ESLint), format (Prettier), type check (tsc), and prevent commits to main. Use husky + lint-staged.
evals.expected_output: Husky and lint-staged configuration with all 4 hooks

assertions.name: husky_setup
assertions.description: Husky installed and configured

assertions.name: lint_staged
assertions.description: lint-staged runs ESLint and Prettier on staged files only

assertions.name: type_check
assertions.description: TypeScript type checking in pre-commit

assertions.name: main_protection
assertions.description: Hook prevents direct commits to main branch

assertions.name: package_json
assertions.description: Scripts added to package.json for hook installation

evals.prompt: Our git history is messy — lots of 'WIP', 'fix typo', 'oops' commits on a feature branch. Clean up the last 8 commits into 3 logical commits before creating a PR.
evals.expected_output: Interactive rebase plan squashing 8 commits into 3 logical groupings

assertions.name: interactive_rebase
assertions.description: Uses git rebase -i to squash/fixup commits

assertions.name: logical_grouping
assertions.description: Groups commits by logical change, not by time

assertions.name: new_messages
assertions.description: New commit messages follow conventional format

assertions.name: verify_after
assertions.description: Tests run after rebase to ensure nothing broke

assertions.name: force_push_warning
assertions.description: Notes need for force push and warns about shared branches
